log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a UPX-compressed PE32 executable tagged as a 'spreader' and 'persistence' threat. I now have a good overview. Next, I should get all IOC types to understand the full scope of indicators

The IOC summary shows no network activity (no IPs, domains, or HTTP), but confirms registry persistence keys and dropped files. The sample is a spreader that creates a deep process tree and drops many

No network activity was captured in any sandbox. This is common for offline spreaders or those that only operate locally. The sample is clearly a local spreader/persistence tool based on the deep proc

The dropped files tool only shows the main executable. However, the initial overview showed 27 files written to the Temp directory (996e_3202.exe through 996e_3202s.exe). I need to examine the process

The process activity shows the main executable spawning a child process with a similar name. The notable modules include wininet.dll, comctl32.dll, kernel32, ntmarta.dll, and rpcrt4.dll. The mutexes l

The registry changes confirm the persistence mechanism. The malware sets a Run key named 'Trickler' pointing to a file in the Downloads folder (likely a dropped payload) and also to a file in the Temp
